runs.synthesis:
# PHASE 6: SYNTHESIS & TODO LIST

## PART 1: Executive Summary

The comprehensive analysis of the Swizzley FX trading system during March 4-5, 2026 reveals **critical systemic failures** requiring immediate intervention. With 1,029 trades analyzed, the system demonstrates a concerning 31.10% win rate and -$5.53 total P&L, indicating negative expectancy that makes continued operation unsustainable.

**The core issue is a massive risk:reward calculation failure** - the system predicts 8.6:1 R:R ratios but delivers only 0.006:1 actual ratios, representing a 99.93% overestimation error. This fundamental pricing model breakdown, combined with disabled critical risk checks (spread monitoring for scalps), creates a perfect storm of poor performance. The system is heavily scalp-dependent (99.8% of trades) with symmetric pricing that ignores market structure, while JPY pairs suffer 50x higher slippage due to pip size handling errors.

**Version analysis reveals significant deployment instability** with win rates swinging from 36.64% to 15.38% across different code versions. The most damaging change was increasing scalp MA score weights from 0.14 to 0.24, which reduced win rates by 9.36%. Conversely, the Printf format fix in swizzley-trader (version 9bd6121f) consistently delivered 34%+ win rates across multiple finmon versions, highlighting execution engine stability as crucial.

**The scoring pipeline shows fundamental component effectiveness issues** with most traditional indicators providing minimal predictive value. Only volatility measures (Bollinger Band Width) show meaningful correlation with winning trades (36% higher volatility in winners), while RSI, Stochastic, and MA alignment show essentially random performance. The current component weighting heavily favors weak predictors (FibStackScore, PatternScore) while underweighting the strongest signal (TrendScore).

**Risk management is critically compromised** with three essential checks disabled, including scalp spread monitoring. The system's symmetric SL/TP distances (~0.00012-0.00013) ignore market dynamics, while premature exits on losing trades (26 minutes average) versus winners (43 minutes) suggest trailing stop logic needs refinement. Position sizing analysis shows optimal performance at 11-20 units, yet the system allows up to 41+ units with degraded results.

## PART 2: TODO List

# PHASE 6: SYNTHESIS & PRIORITIZED TODO LIST

## PART 1: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The Swizzley FX system is in **critical condition** with a devastating 25.68% win rate and -$11.70 total P&L across 553 trades. The analysis reveals a system that is fundamentally broken across multiple dimensions, but with clear pathways to recovery.

**Key Findings**: The scalp timeframe dominates trading volume (98% of trades) but is hemorrhaging money with a -$9.84 loss. However, the recent version deployment (finmon b5698030 + trader 9bd6121f) showed a remarkable **19.23 percentage point improvement** to 35.90% win rate, proving the system can be profitable. Critical risk guardrails are disabled, allowing poor-quality trades to execute unchecked.

**Root Causes**: The scoring system fails to predict profitable trades, with higher scores showing no correlation with better performance. Risk management is severely compromised with spread checks, pip distance limits, and position limits all disabled. The system trades too frequently (15-minute cooldowns) with inadequate R:R requirements (1.5x minimum) for the current win rate.

**Immediate Opportunity**: The latest code version demonstrates the system's potential, but protective measures must be implemented immediately to prevent further losses while optimization continues. The intraday timeframe shows promise (37.50% win rate, +$1.03 P&L) but needs larger sample sizes.

**Strategic Direction**: Focus on protecting and understanding the recent performance improvement while implementing critical risk controls. The system needs immediate defensive measures followed by systematic optimization of the scoring pipeline and risk management framework.

## PART 2: PRIORITIZED TODO LIST

todos.title: Fix Critical R:R Calculation Model
todos.description: The system predicts 8.6:1 R:R but delivers 0.006:1 actual ratios (99.93% error). This fundamental pricing model failure is the primary cause of poor performance despite reasonable win rates.
todos.expected_impact: 5-10% win rate improvement by fixing position sizing and SL/TP calculations
todos.evidence: Phase 4: Predicted R:R 8.59 vs actual 0.006 for scalps, representing 1,400x overestimation

todos.title: Enable Disabled Critical Risk Checks
todos.description: Three critical risk checks are disabled: scalp spread monitoring, intraday confirmation, and swing confirmation. This creates massive risk exposure.
todos.expected_impact: 2-5% win rate improvement by filtering poor setups
todos.evidence: Phase 4: check.spread.scalp, check.confirm.intraday, check.confirm.swing all disabled in settings analysis

todos.title: Fix JPY Pair Slippage Handling
todos.description: JPY pairs show 50x higher slippage (0.051 vs 0.001) due to pip size calculation errors. This severely impacts trade profitability.
todos.evidence: Phase 4: NZD_JPY 0.0513 slippage vs EUR_USD 0.001 slippage, indicating pip size mishandling

todos.title: Revert MA Score Weight Increase
todos.description: Finmon version 489ac7a0 increased scalp MA score weight from 0.14 to 0.24, causing 9.36% win rate drop. This change should be immediately reverted.
todos.expected_impact: 9% win rate recovery based on version analysis
todos.evidence: Phase 2: Version 489ac7a0 showed 25.58% win rate vs 34.94% in previous version 1db0d637

todos.title: Raise FinalScore and Confidence Thresholds
todos.description: Current thresholds allow trading at FinalScore 0+. Analysis shows optimal performance at FinalScore >30 AND Confidence >0.7 (34.2% win rate vs 31.1% overall).
todos.expected_impact: 3% win rate improvement with 79% trade volume reduction
todos.evidence: Phase 3: FinalScore >30 AND Confidence >0.7 shows 34.2% win rate (199 trades) vs 31.1% overall

todos.title: Reweight Components Based on Effectiveness
todos.description: Increase weights for effective components (TrendScore, MaScore, CrossoverScore) and decrease weights for ineffective ones (FibStackScore, PatternScore, VolumeScore).
todos.expected_impact: 2-4% win rate improvement by emphasizing predictive components
todos.evidence: Phase 3: TrendScore r=0.074 (strongest), VolumeScore r=0.020 (weakest), yet current weights don't reflect effectiveness

todos.title: Optimize Position Sizing to 11-20 Units
todos.description: Analysis shows optimal performance at 11-20 units (30.4% win rate) vs 26.7% for 41+ units. Reduce maximum position size and default to optimal range.
todos.expected_impact: 1-2% win rate improvement by avoiding oversized positions
todos.evidence: Phase 4: 11-20 units show 30.4% win rate vs 26.7% for 41+ units, with r=-0.029 correlation

todos.title: Implement Asymmetric SL/TP Pricing
todos.description: Replace symmetric 0.00012-0.00013 SL/TP distances with market-adaptive levels. Winners held 43min vs losers 26min suggests better TP placement needed.
todos.expected_impact: 2-3% win rate improvement through better risk:reward ratios
todos.evidence: Phase 4: Symmetric pricing ignores market structure, winners held 43.4min vs losers 25.8min

todos.title: Increase VolatilityScore Weighting
todos.description: Bollinger Band Width shows strongest predictive value (36% higher in winners). Increase VolatilityScore weight from 8 to 12-15.
todos.expected_impact: 1-2% win rate improvement by emphasizing most predictive indicator
todos.evidence: Phase 5: Bollinger Width 0.0638 in winners vs 0.0468 in losers (36% difference, strongest signal)

todos.title: Implement Session-Based Trading Filters
todos.description: London morning hours (6-8 UTC) show 35-40% win rates vs 25-30% during NY afternoon (14-16 UTC). Implement session-based trading restrictions.
todos.expected_impact: 1-2% win rate improvement by avoiding poor trading hours
todos.evidence: Phase 5: Best hours 6-8 UTC (35-40% win rate), worst hours 14-16 UTC (25-30% win rate)

todos.title: Investigate Intraday/Swing Confirmation Failure
todos.description: Only 2 intraday trades (0.2%) and 0 swing trades suggest confirmation requirements are too restrictive, preventing higher timeframe diversification.
todos.expected_impact: 0.5-1% win rate improvement through better timeframe distribution
todos.evidence: Phase 1: 1,027 scalp (99.8%) vs 2 intraday (0.2%) vs 0 swing trades shows extreme concentration

todos.title: Review PatternScore Calculation Logic
todos.description: Pattern trades show 30.5% win rate with negative average scores for both winners (-0.19) and losers (-0.40), suggesting pattern recognition adds noise rather than signal.
todos.expected_impact: 0.5-1% win rate improvement by fixing or removing noisy patterns
todos.evidence: Phase 5: 771 pattern trades (75%) with 30.5% win rate, both winners and losers have negative PatternScores

todos.title: Analyze Symbol-Specific Performance Drivers
todos.description: CHF pairs outperform (GBP_CHF 42.86% win rate) while major USD pairs underperform (EUR_USD 24.49%). Need deeper analysis of currency-specific factors.
todos.evidence: Phase 1: GBP_CHF +$0.05 P&L vs EUR_GBP -$0.29 P&L, CHF pairs consistently top performers

todos.title: Implement Version Stability Monitoring
todos.description: Version transitions show extreme performance swings (36.64% to 15.38% win rates). Need automated performance monitoring for deployments.
todos.evidence: Phase 2: 9 version combinations with win rates ranging from 15.38% to 36.64%

todos.title: Reduce OscillatorScore Weighting
todos.description: RSI and Stochastic RSI show no predictive value (0.13 and 0.5 point differences between wins/losses). Reduce OscillatorScore weight from 10 to 6-8.
todos.evidence: Phase 5: RSI winners 50.30 vs losers 50.17 (0.13 difference), Stochastic RSI 48.03 vs 47.52 (0.5 difference)
